utility_evaluation.REASONING:
There are 5 items requested in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 items appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details:
- health_coverage: Requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Plan provides physical and mental components and a combined cost (€75/month). Although individual line items have plan names and a bundled total, it does not break out separate costs per physical vs mental beyond the stated plan labels with implied costs in parentheses; however, the parentheses do specify €45 and €30 respectively, so costs are provided. This is fully specified.
- property_coverage: Requires home/contents insurance with costs. Provided: InsurePlus Standard Coverage with mention of equipment protection up to €3000 and cost €60/month. Fully specified.
- travel_coverage: Requires travel insurance with costs. Provided: InsurePlus Standard Plan including adventure activities and cost €50/month. Fully specified.
- additional_services: Requires any enhancement services with costs. Provided as "N/A"; while it indicates none selected, it still addresses the item. This counts as specified.
- total_budget: Requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Provided: €185/month and €2220/year. Fully specified.
All 5 items are present and sufficiently detailed per the format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists specific product names. I matched each to the exact entries in the ground-truth ratings. For health coverage, it includes SecureLife Basic Plan for both physical and mental; these correspond to ratings under primary_health_coverage.physical_health and primary_health_coverage.mental_health. For property coverage, it lists InsurePlus Standard Coverage under home_content. For travel, it lists InsurePlus Standard Plan under travel_insurance. I then extracted the exact ratings for those items.
